examples: Salyut 1 was built as a place where people could live while they observed space and did experiments.
Its creation was a corporation "Russian system" of the treaty with MMPP Salyut.
The Soviet Salyut 6 returned to earth; its cosmonauts had been in space for a record 185 days.
In 1978, two Soviet cosmonauts returned to Earth from the Salyut 6 space station after setting a new endurance record of 139 days, 14 hours.
In 1991 Mir's predecessor Salyut 7 plunged into the Andes Mountains.
